prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "平板支撑" (forearm plank) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person holding a forearm plank: forearms flat on the ground with elbows directly under the shoulders, forearms parallel to the body, hands relaxed; toes on the ground, feet hip-width apart; body in a perfectly straight line from heels to head with no sag at the hips and no piking up; hips at the same height as shoulders; head in line with the spine, neutral neck, eyes looking down; glutes engaged, core braced; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: rectus abdominis (front of abdomen) and transverse abdominis (deep core). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus, quadriceps, and shoulder stabilizers (serratus anterior). Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腹直肌" pointing to the rectus abdominis; "腹横肌" pointing to the transverse abdominis (deep core); "臀肌 / 股四头肌" pointing to the glute and quadriceps stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
